summary:
**US-Iran hostilities** re-escalated sharply today after Iran downed a US Apache helicopter, prompting **Trump** to announce resumed strikes and warning Iran to "pay the price." **WTI oil recovered above $90** on the headline, adding to inflation concerns already on high alert ahead of the CPI print.

**May CPI came in at 4.2% YoY** (in-line) with a softer-than-expected core of **0.2% MoM vs. 0.3% expected** — the first MoM drop in core goods since May 2025. Views diverged sharply: **AndreasSteno** declared the inflation shock over and called it good news for the Warsh outlook, while **saxena_puru**, **Geiger_Capital**, and **BobEUnlimited** warned that 4.2% headline is not cause for celebration and a hawkish FOMC remains the likely outcome. **Matt_Hougan** noted CME rate expectations didn't move at all on the print, suggesting it was fully priced.

The **SpaceX IPO** ($250B demand, 4x oversubscribed) dominated equity market narrative, with **jimcramer** and **aleabitoreddit** observing broad tech and crypto liquidation to fund the offering. **Delphi_Digital** published a detailed analysis projecting **60x compression in launch costs by 2045** and mapping the economic thresholds each price drop unlocks, from megaconstellations to a lunar economy.

**Claude Fable 5** (Anthropic's latest frontier model) launched today and immediately generated controversy: **dylan522p** reported OpenAI gained usage share the same day due to Fable safety refusals frustrating power users; **loomdart** found a workaround via prompt phrasing; and **cryptopunk7213** described a novel malware vector exploiting Fable's safety classifier design. **hooeem** posted a detailed two-prompt workflow for AI-assisted planning that circulated widely.

**Crypto markets** under broad pressure: **BTC** around $61k, **ETH** at $1,615, **SOL** at $63 per **rektmando's** daily digest. **novogratz** reported heading to DC for crypto Clarity Act lobbying, calling the next four weeks the last window before summer recess and election season. **beaniemaxi** published a structural critique of **Strategy (MSTR)** calling the model a VC ponzi requiring infinite valuation to work, while **alpha_pls** argued large-cap crypto "vapour" projects with no revenue are finally trending toward zero.

A_events.cluster: US-Iran Escalation: Helicopter Downed, Strikes Resumed

items.author: JavierBlas
items.summary: Breaking news anchor: Trump announced the US will resume attacking Iran 'very hard' as soon as 'today,' citing the downing of a US helicopter. Trump added Iran should sign a deal and expressed frustration at their negotiating pace: 'I don't know what they're doing.' Earlier he quoted Iran taking 'too long' and said they will 'pay the price.' Separately, rektmando's daily digest noted Iran fired back at US military bases and Trump also floated an Iran rebuild offer in exchange for half its oil, with reports Iran may accept a 15-year enrichment halt.

items.author: elerianm
items.summary: WTI oil bounced back above $90 in response to Trump's statement, after briefly dipping. El-Erian notes this adds to an ongoing debate between two camps: those expecting a price collapse due to a supply glut vs. those forecasting a price surge as inventories reach critically low levels. Chart attached showing the intraday oil volatility.

items.author: Geiger_Capital
items.summary: Multiple posts reacting to the Iran escalation. Called out Trump's contradiction: 'Trump chose Kevin Warsh to cut rates and then immediately started a new Middle East war, forcing him to hike rates even higher.' Separately shared the headline 'TRUMP: WILL BE ATTACKING IRAN HARD' with the comment 'Can we please leave the Middle East.' Also commented on the political response to the Belfast beheading, calling the reaction from certain leaders 'pathetic and weak.'

items.author: _tolks
items.summary: Claims the chart setup predicted the escalation before the helicopter news broke, calling it visible 'if you were mentally ill enough to see it.' On equity direction: says the selloff to current levels was predictable, but now at levels where anyone bullish on AI or the singularity should be bidding hard — 'no bottom here is cooked for months or more.' Took profits on yolo calls added yesterday, now mostly observing after the bounce rather than holding max short.

items.author: zriboua
items.summary: Observes that Trump's negotiating style is consistent: his version of a deal is always the other party capitulating entirely. Characterizes the failed Iran talks as 'No deal episode 671839.' Attaches a chart showing the breakdown. Also separately shared a Substack piece on anti-Zionism and third-worldism framing.

A_events.cluster: May CPI Print: 4.2% Headline, 0.2% Core MoM — Market Reaction Split

items.author: elerianm
items.summary: Pre-release preview focused on whether headline inflation is spilling into core three months after the initial energy surge. Highlighted that the consensus expected 0.5% MoM headline (4.2% YoY) and 0.3% core MoM (2.9% YoY). Post-release: confirmed headline in line, core softer than expected, 'suggesting limited spillover in May.' His watch point was the pipeline for broader inflation if/when the energy shock fades. Also separately flagged China's widening gap between CPI (1.2%) and PPI (3.9%), attributing it to consumer price resistance and moral suasion.

items.author: Geiger_Capital
items.summary: Posted the CPI scorecard (CPI MoM 0.5% vs 0.5% exp; Core MoM 0.2% vs 0.3% exp; CPI YoY 4.2% vs 4.2% exp; Core YoY 2.9% vs 2.9% exp) with the headline 'Inflation back over 4%.' Separately argued that if income has not risen at least 30% since COVID, 'you are now poorer' using government data, with the real number likely 40%+ — attaching a chart of cumulative price levels.

items.author: AndreasSteno
items.summary: Called it 'fairly benign' excluding energy: shelter costs in core came in hotter than penciled, but core goods pricing is rolling over and there is 'very limited evidence of any second-order effects from the Hormuz crisis.' Concluded inflation has peaked and will be reflected in data over coming months. Says this report 'will alleviate some of the worst concerns that Kevin Warsh will turn imminently hawkish.' His NowcastIQ model hit headline at 0.45% and core at 0.24% — within a few basis points. Flagged earlier pre-print that a hawkish number would be 'a bloodbath.'

items.author: Fullcarry
items.summary: Noted there haven't been many big CPI surprises lately. Flagged the 10-year ahead of the UST 10y auction: the 5s10s30s butterfly has diverged recently with 10s richening, meaning the belly looks too rich relative to the wings (2s and 30s). Chart attached showing dual-axis 5s10s30s fly vs term premium proxy.

items.author: saxena_puru
items.summary: Called the initial market rally on a 4.2% YoY print a 'strange reaction' — his view is this is not cause for celebration and a hawkish FOMC remains the likely outcome. Disclosed roughly 50% cash positioning, still expecting turbulence over the next few weeks, and characterized the market as still overbought.

items.author: Matt_Hougan
items.summary: Argued the CPI result was fully priced: CME December rate hike/cut odds did not move at all on the print, citing the CME FedWatch chart as evidence the market had already absorbed the inflation reality a month ago.

items.author: Swedish_uranium
items.summary: Flagged the first MoM drop in core goods CPI since May 2025 as a positive catalyst for silver, gold, uranium, and equities. Shared the full scorecard at release and noted silver ticked up $1 immediately, expecting a continued relief rally.

A_events.cluster: SpaceX IPO: 4x Oversubscribed, Market Liquidation Effect

items.author: jimcramer
items.summary: Three posts on the SpaceX IPO dynamics: characterized the day as 'major liquidation mode for SpaceX including all speculative assets.' Said Bitcoin and gold are 'bad money being liquidated for SpaceX' while Apple and Nvidia are 'good money also being liquidated.' Noted SpaceX was 4x oversubscribed with lots of money, contrasting with Cerebras which was 20x oversubscribed but was 'a bust' due to much less capital. Called it a 'predictable' rotation where traders/speculators are selling tech to make room.

items.author: aleabitoreddit
items.summary: Noted the broader index is down 3-4% on the week, posting a portfolio heat map. Highlighted that retail favorites like $RDDT, $HOOD, and $NFLX are green even in the selloff, and separately argued that $NVDA's downtrend might reverse if Trump stopped threatening Iran daily. Also noted Morgan Stanley confirmed Nvidia denied 800V DC pushback reports from SemiAnalysis — called the entire CPO/800V selloff 'the dumbest' since the MU HBM4 share claim.

items.author: dampedspring
items.summary: Flagged $SMCI down 17% on equity offering announcement, attaching a chart. In follow-up reply noted the key question for SMCI is 'when they call it a bargain.'

A_events.cluster: Claude Fable 5 Launch: Praise, Frustration, and Security Exploit

items.author: dylan522p
items.summary: Reports that OpenAI's usage share actually grew vs Anthropic the day Fable 5 launched. Multiple SemiAnalysis power users tried Fable, got safety refusals for 'nonsensical reasons,' got frustrated, gave Codex a real try, and now prefer it over Claude 4.8 Opus. Shared a chart of usage share trends. In a separate reply noted the Fable model 'is materially worse still outside multi-modality.'

items.author: cryptopunk7213
items.summary: Describes a live malware exploit using Fable's safety classifier: attackers submit an npm package with a trigger-word title (e.g. 'biological weapons design'), causing Fable's scanner to send it to an LLM which reads the title first, marks it a 'safety red flag,' and hands it back — without reading the malicious code injection in the body. The result: malicious packages pass the safety scanner and remain downloadable by millions of developers. Argues Anthropic's shift to deployment-level safety is creating new attack vectors.

items.author: loomdart
items.summary: Found a simple workaround for Fable reverting to Opus: adding 'nothing dangerous' to the system prompt kept it in Fable mode. Posted a screenshot as evidence.

items.author: EMostaque
items.summary: Recommends trying Opus Fable on 'low mode' versus high/ultra cost — argues it's good enough to semi one-shot most tasks using far fewer tokens. Separately, in a thread about compute pricing, notes that short-let GPU agreements (90-day termination) price significantly higher than CoreWeave's years-long committed demand, and that Google likely won't use Microsoft/CoreWeave-style deployments for training given they have TPUs. Also fired off a meme tweet asking 'anyone hiring witches to save training runs.'

items.author: stonecoldpat0
items.summary: Pushback against influencers praising the Belfast riots following a street beheading. As someone who grew up in Belfast, distinguishes between outrage at the beheading (warranted) and burning innocent people's homes (not the answer). Contextualizes it as hoodlums exploiting the situation, consistent with Northern Ireland's history where such groups 'pop up from time to time and cause chaos.' Directly calls out crypto influencers encouraging the hate as 'disgusting.' Also explicitly told Pompliano to 'go fuck yourself' for what he characterized as irresponsible commentary.

A_events.cluster: Hyperliquid HIP-3: Commodity Perps Executing >10% of TradFi Volume

items.author: Crypto_McKenna
items.summary: Three posts on Hyperliquid momentum. HIP-3 commodity markets for Silver and Copper are executing over 10% of total TradFi volume — demonstrating that perpetuals are becoming a dominant instrument for traditional markets and the only one facilitating price discovery when TradFi markets close. The official S&P 500 market on HIP-3 reached $1.15B in daily volume with $455M open interest — a 'significant moment.' Also argues HYPE at $12.91B market cap is underpriced: post-AQAv2 the protocol earns $800-900M annually in revenue with 99% directed to a secondary buyback — unprecedented across digital assets and public companies globally. IPOs of SpaceX, Anthropic, and OpenAI won't be a massive drag on indices since companies need one year before S&P500 inclusion.

A_events.cluster: Neura Robotics Raises $1.4B — Largest Robotics Round Ever

items.author: sjdedic
items.summary: Announces Neura Robotics closing a $1.4B round — the biggest robotics funding round ever, tied with Skild AI, and one of the largest European tech rounds in history. Investors include Tether, Amazon, and Nvidia. Sjdedic invested via Moonrock and the echo.xyz community. Frames it as a geopolitical arbitrage: while everyone focuses on the US-China robotics race, 'when two quarrel, the third rejoices' — positioning Europe as the beneficiary.

A_events.cluster: Crypto Clarity Act: Novogratz Goes to DC, 4-Week Window

items.author: novogratz
items.summary: Mike Novogratz heading to Washington for a full day of lobbying meetings on the Clarity Act. Remains optimistic the bill can pass, calling it 'very complicated legislation' now down to two or three unresolved issues with seemingly fair solutions. Frames the next four weeks as the only remaining window — after that, summer recess followed by increasingly divisive election season. Acknowledges the unequal burden of change and praises the DC-based industry advocates for their 'patience and persistence.'

B_insights.author: BobEUnlimited
B_insights.summary: Published a Substack piece titled 'Welcome Back 4% Inflation' arguing this cycle is structurally different from post-COVID: wage growth is slowing, there is no fiscal support coming, and households are being squeezed rather than boosted. Followed up with a chart showing real wage growth is negative. In a separate thread responding to Jack Farley, estimated non-Iranian dark oil supply movement at 1-2M bbl/d — citing JPM analysis — and characterized China's situation as mostly drawing down inventories with some demand destruction, observable via a 5M bbl/d drop in imports since onshore stock reporting is opaque. Also critiqued real-time inflation trackers like Truflation for implausibly showing current inflation below December 2025 levels during an active oil shock.

B_insights.author: Delphi_Digital
B_insights.summary: Comprehensive analysis of the SpaceX IPO's broader economic implications. Launch costs barely moved for 60 years until SpaceX cut them by an order of magnitude; Horace Dediu (formerly of the Christensen Institute) projects another 60x drop by 2045. Maps specific price thresholds to industries they unlock: $1,500/kg enables megaconstellations and Earth observation (already done); $500/kg opens orbital compute and commercial stations; $200/kg opens microgravity manufacturing; $50/kg unlocks a lunar economy; $10/kg underpins the Mars supply chain. Starlink already carries capacity equivalent to up to 20% of real-time global internet traffic. Orbital data centers — science fiction in 2019 — are now funded in response to terrestrial power constraints. Anthropic and Google signed deals paying SpaceX ~$26B/year for access to Colossus on the ground. Concludes: 'The most interesting founders in the sector are already building for launch costs that don't exist yet.'

B_insights.author: hooeem
B_insights.summary: Published a widely-circulated three-part thread detailing a two-prompt AI planning workflow for working with Claude Code or Codex. Prompt 1 is the 'Karpathy prompt' — a system instruction demanding complete, provocative, non-validating answers with explicit confidence levels and no capitulation without new evidence. Prompt 2 is a 'plan prompt by Matt Pocock' — a structured interview framework that builds a design tree, identifies critical path decisions, locks a spec before building, and requires stopping mid-execution if unexpected decisions arise. The workflow: use the two prompts with a cheaper thinking model (e.g. Opus 4.6 or 5.5) to fully resolve the design before handing a locked spec to a cheaper executor like Fable. Frames it as staying 'the human in the loop' and avoiding costly agentic token loops.

B_insights.author: beaniemaxi
B_insights.summary: Structural critique of Strategy (MSTR): describes the model as 'essentially VC ponzi funding' — using illiquid equity in an unprofitable company to constantly raise and inject capital, diluting themselves with their own capital every time. Argues it 'only works if the valuation goes to infinity.' Attaches a chart illustrating the dilution mechanics. Also took a separate shot at Solana for promoting a sports sponsorship while ignoring FastdotPoker, which he describes as the first fully on-chain, encrypted, permissionless poker protocol on Solana — arguing the foundation fails builders by prioritizing marketing over real apps.

B_insights.author: alpha_pls
B_insights.summary: Extended argument that the current era is when large-cap crypto 'vapour' finally trends to actual zero. Cites Cardano at $7B FDV as 'utterly ridiculous.' Argues retail will not return to legacy L1s for the next cycle because they now have frontier equity assets (AI, robotics, SpaceX) offering better multiples. Six years is long enough to prove a thesis — legacy L1s haven't delivered and everyone holding since the 2020 pump is at a loss. Frames the long-term trade as: long revenue-producing crypto assets with value accrual, short historical infra vapour. Notes the number of CEX new coin listings has also dramatically dropped since Q1 2026 as teams avoid risk-off launches.

B_insights.author: patrick_oshag
B_insights.summary: Shared an analysis on technology adoption S-curves: radio is the third-fastest tech to reach 50% penetration in history (slower only than AI and the smartphone), but the dishwasher took 47 years. Frames AI as having two adoption paths: the 'radio model' (direct consumer access via browser, drives fast adoption) vs. the 'dishwasher model' (B2B enterprise requiring integration into existing systems, historically 20 years delayed as seen with cloud). Key bottleneck for enterprise AI: IT security concerns, cultural resistance, and lack of evangelical leaders — exactly what happened with cloud until the CIA and Capital One publicly validated it. Notes a 'backwards L-curve' in AI consumer adoption that is historically unprecedented.

B_insights.author: paulg
B_insights.summary: Reports a concrete AI economics data point: spoke with an AI startup founder generating roughly a 40% annual return on the cost of his GPU hardware — i.e., $400 in annual revenue per $1,000 of hardware. Shared without further commentary, implying it as a benchmark for early AI infrastructure economics.

B_insights.author: qthomp
B_insights.summary: Argues policymaker reaction functions are 'the most underappreciated force in markets.' Draws parallels between oil-to-$200 bulls and AI singularity bulls — both ignore how politicians respond when things get uncomfortable. Framed as a structural mental model for why extrapolation trades fail.

B_insights.author: sjdedic
B_insights.summary: Contrarian take on the perceived death of early-stage crypto VC: argues the chart everyone reads as 'end of crypto VC' actually signals an unprecedented buying opportunity. Four arguments: (1) least competitive early-stage environment since 2019 means no bidding wars or inflated terms; (2) quality-to-capital ratio has never been more attractive, with founders raising at floor valuations; (3) AI means a 5-person team ships what used to require 50, so seed dollars go further; (4) capital is no longer the differentiator — hands-on partnership is, and barely anyone provides it. Moonrock is doubling down on early stage.

B_insights.author: thedefiedge
B_insights.summary: Methodology post: watches what bounces fastest in a crash as an accumulation signal. In the current downturn, 7 of the top 14 fastest-recovering tokens came from one sector. Full list with FDVs and bounce percentages: Velvet Capital ($165M, +71%), ReadyCards ($14M, +44%), xMaquina ($18M, +37%), Stargate ($41M, +29%), Zcash ($7.2B, +27%), Keeta Network ($99M, +21%), World Network ($1.7B, +21%), NEAR ($2.7B, +15%), Pump.fun ($529M, +14%), Hyperliquid ($12.6B, +13%), Ondo Finance ($1.7B, +12%), Bittensor ($2B, +11%), USDai ($74M, +11%), Unibase AI ($355M, +10%). Notes conditions are still too choppy to bid but builds his watchlist from this data.

B_insights.author: laurashin
B_insights.summary: Podcast promotion for Bits and Bips featuring @ramahluwalia's thesis: the consensus frames Strategy (MSTR) as a Bitcoin price problem, but ramahluwalia argues the company was built specifically for a Minsky market phase (euphoric credit expansion) that has already ended — which materially changes the analytical question from 'will BTC go up' to 'is this business model viable in a normal market.' Also quoted Austin Campbell's math showing that owning Strategy common stock pencils out to a ~9% funding cost just to hold Bitcoin, making unlevered ETF ownership more efficient.

B_insights.author: aleabitoreddit
B_insights.summary: Ongoing thread on photonics/optical semiconductor recovery. $LITE, $AAOI, and $SIVE recovering after what he called a 'stupid' initial selloff. Expects European supply chain names ($SOI, $XFAB, $IQE) to follow. Also flagged an institutional accumulation pattern in $SIVE (Sivers Semiconductors): Blackrock entered passively after MSCI/Nasdaq index listing, and Fidelity Research (active arm) has started direct positions — following the pattern where JPMorgan appeared last month with small positions and then bought ~5.25% of the company. Frames this as US institutions validating Sivers's photonics position and accumulating, cycling from local Swedish investors to US investors to US institutions.

B_insights.author: goodalexander
B_insights.summary: Argues there is no universal definition of 'good' for AI models — each has different strengths: Claude is better for design and wordcel tasks, Codex is better for backend engineering, DeepSeek is cheap and good at document parsing but runs too hot (high temperature). The only consistent finding: nothing competitive out of Europe.

B_insights.author: stacy_muur
B_insights.summary: Observed that Lido Finance lost ~26% TVL in the last 30 days — nearly exactly matching ETH's 28% price drop from ~$2,306 to ~$1,649 — attributing it to the broader Ethereum Foundation identity crisis rather than Lido-specific issues. When ETH drops, users exit positions and TVL follows mechanically. Also shared a bearish frame: a recent Solana development (image attached) implies no memecoins, which she calls 'very bearish for all memecoin infrastructure.'

B_insights.author: Tyler_Neville_
B_insights.summary: Shared a chart described as 'incredible' showing the last 20 years as a 'giant debt-for-equity swap' — possibly coming to an end. Separately commented on Jamie Dimon's opposition to bank competition, quoting someone who exposed record net interest margins and falling deposit rates being dressed up as 'consumer protection.' His caption: 'I am a free-market capitalist, until it comes to innovation in my industry — then I get what I want through political pressure.'

B_insights.author: Chartfest1
B_insights.summary: Observed that penny stock trading is back and currently 3 stocks account for 25% of trading on the Nasdaq. Linked to Yahoo Finance's most active penny stocks screener. Attributes it partly to prior pattern (trading penny stocks in Sept/Oct and again in Feb) rather than solely the recent PDT rule change. In separate replies recommended commodities as a sector focus.

C_contrarian.author: AndreasSteno
C_contrarian.summary: Against the prevailing hawkish CPI narrative: declares 'the inflation shock is over (unless the war restarts).' While the market and most commentators focused on 4.2% headline inflation as alarming, Steno calls the report 'fairly benign' — points to rolling-over core goods, limited Hormuz crisis pass-through, and his NowcastIQ model nailing the print within a few basis points. Predicts Warsh will not turn 'imminently hawkish' and that inflation will slowly improve in coming months.

C_contrarian.author: Matt_Hougan
C_contrarian.summary: Against the view that CPI was a market-moving event: argues the inflation news was fully priced in a month ago. CME December rate probabilities did not move at all on the print — suggesting anyone reacting to today's number as new information was already behind.

C_contrarian.author: qthomp
C_contrarian.summary: Against both extreme oil bulls and AI singularity bulls: argues both camps systematically ignore policymaker reaction functions. When markets get disruptive enough, politicians intervene — making $200 oil and unconstrained AI dominance politically unsustainable extrapolations regardless of supply/demand or technology fundamentals.

C_contrarian.author: sjdedic
C_contrarian.summary: Against the consensus view that early-stage crypto VC is dead: argues the very chart everyone cites as evidence of the sector's collapse actually shows the best buying opportunity since 2019. Everyone midcurving the decline misses that less capital chasing quality founders means better terms, lower valuations, and more productive seed dollars in the AI era.

C_contrarian.author: ZeeContrarian1
C_contrarian.summary: Calls ARKK's ~35% decline over the same period that $QQQ advanced a case of 'incompetence on the highest possible scale.' Frames the last few years as potentially 'among the most important periods of innovation in human history' driven by AI — and yet the fund explicitly named 'Innovation ETF' massively underperformed. Chart attached comparing the two.

C_contrarian.author: udiWertheimer
C_contrarian.summary: Published a draft he held back, arguing Strategy (MSTR/strc) has to implode — eventually. Not necessarily now, and Bitcoin could go up a lot first. But the structure is unsustainable: when it does implode, 'it will be very ugly.' Frames the delay as a timing question, not a structural one. Notes he previously held back this view because he didn't want to be reactionary but stands by the core thesis.

C_contrarian.author: Crypto_McKenna
C_contrarian.summary: Against the fear that upcoming mega-IPOs (SpaceX, Anthropic, OpenAI) will drain indices: argues these companies won't be a massive drag because S&P500 requires one year post-listing before inclusion under committee approval, limiting forced rebalancing. The IPOs may act as temporary liquidity events but not structural index headwinds.

D_positioning.author: saxena_puru
D_positioning.summary: Disclosed approximately 50% cash positioning following the CPI print. Still expecting market turbulence over the next few weeks and considers the market overbought. Hawkish FOMC remains his base case despite the initial post-CPI rally.

D_positioning.author: Fullcarry
D_positioning.summary: Rates curve positioning: does not like the belly of the curve (5s and 10s too rich relative to wings — 2s and 30s). The 5s10s30s butterfly has diverged recently with 10s richening up, while the fly generally tracks term premium. Expressed disdain for the belly ahead of the UST 10y auction.

D_positioning.author: lord_fed
D_positioning.summary: Posted several option-related charts and a live trade comment: 'I don't think we sell like yesterday and Friday today… kinda wanna fade this dip into close but already have a trade on for Friday. If I had to pick a strike for today probably try the 7390c 0d here @ 4.1.' Chart images attached showing intraday SPX setups.

D_positioning.author: aleabitoreddit
D_positioning.summary: Maintains positions in photonics/optical semiconductor names: $LITE, $AAOI, $SIVE, $AXTI, $ALRIB, $LPK. Noted AXT ($AXTI) has dilution concerns and newer float structure risks. Holds $SOI, $XFAB, $IQE in the European supply chain. Also watching $NVDA after Morgan Stanley confirmed the 800V DC delay reports are incorrect per Nvidia itself.

D_positioning.author: thedefivillain
D_positioning.summary: Flagged $BEAT ($6B FDV) as having the potential to become the next $LAB/$RAVE squeeze — a 'trapped shorts' setup. Dollar OI is close to $180M, suggesting heavy short interest. Funding has not gone negative yet (unusual for a 'scam coin' at this valuation). Historical pattern: each time this setup appeared at similar valuations, the coin went to $20B FDV afterward before collapsing. Also noted $STG is now 5x the price of $ZRO (chart attached).

D_positioning.author: Swedish_uranium
D_positioning.summary: Called the CPI print bullish for silver, gold, uranium, and equities — specifically the first MoM drop in core goods since May 2025. Covers the uranium sector under a prospect generator framework via Skyharbour Resources ($SYH / $SYH.V). Note: disclosure of paid partnership with Skyharbour included.

D_positioning.author: Crypto_McKenna
D_positioning.summary: Long HYPE thesis: Hyperliquid at $12.91B market cap earns $800-900M annually in revenue post-AQAv2. 99% of protocol revenue feeds a secondary HYPE buyback — a capital return policy unprecedented across digital assets and public companies. With HIP-3 markets for S&P500, Silver, and Copper hitting significant daily volume milestones, considers HYPE underpriced at current levels.

D_positioning.author: _tolks
D_positioning.summary: Exited the majority of yolo calls added for the Friday/next-week expiry after the post-escalation bounce. Less uncertain on direction now. Would have high conviction max short but crypto/equity markets 'are the most perfectly designed uponly scams' — reverted to mostly observing.

E_links.author: BobEUnlimited
E_links.summary: Substack essay: 'Welcome Back 4% Inflation' — argues this inflation surge is structurally different from post-COVID boom, with slowing wages, no fiscal support, and households being squeezed rather than stimulated.

E_links.author: laurashin
E_links.summary: Bits and Bips podcast episode featuring ramahluwalia's thesis that Strategy was built for a Minsky market phase that has ended, and Austin Campbell's math showing ~9% funding cost for holding Bitcoin via MSTR common stock vs. just buying the ETF.

E_links.author: Chartfest1
E_links.summary: Yahoo Finance most active penny stocks screener — referenced while noting that 3 penny stocks now account for 25% of daily Nasdaq volume.

E_links.author: jvisserlabs
E_links.summary: Market webinar on AI infrastructure themes and navigating the AI mid-cycle slowdown — promises to demonstrate a Jensen Huang knowledge brain tool and upload a how-to document for building a custom LLM-powered investment brain.

E_links.author: rektmando
E_links.summary: Daily crypto and macro digest subscription link — covering BTC at $61k, ETH ETF outflows, SpaceX IPO 4x oversubscribed, Fable 5 launch, CME Nasdaq/BTC/ETH/SOL futures listing, Japan megabanks joint stablecoin, Morpho $175M raise, EU ban on Russian platforms, and more.

E_links.author: patrick_oshag
E_links.summary: Chart from Nikunj illustrating the 'backwards L-curve' in AI adoption — contrasting the radio-style fast consumer S-curve vs. the dishwasher-style slow B2B integration curve, with historical penetration data from Horace Dediu.

E_links.author: Swedish_uranium
E_links.summary: Silver price chart referenced to show the immediate positive reaction to the soft core CPI reading.

E_links.author: RayDalio
E_links.summary: Ray Dalio posted a link with no commentary — content unknown as URL was not resolved, but given the day's macro context (CPI, Iran escalation, debt dynamics) likely related to his debt cycle framework.

E_links.author: Tyler_Neville_
E_links.summary: Chart showing the past 20 years as a 'giant debt-for-equity swap' — referenced in the context of whether this structural dynamic may be ending.
